line_1: Now, nearer points of spears, and many a cone
line_2: Of moving helmets, in the moonlight shone,

line_1: Upon the topmost mountain's burning cone;
line_2: A shadow stern and sad: he stood alone,

line_1: Above the plumes, upon his helmet's cone,
Upon the highest palm-tree's feathery cone;
line_2: And only its last light yet lingering shone,

line_1: Such reas'ning falls like an inverted cone,
line_2: Wanting its proper base to stand upon.

line_1: 'Mid the gay towers on steep Din's Branna's cone,
line_2: O! Harp of Cambria, never hast thou known

line_1: Bleak as they stand upon their steepy cone,
line_2: Thus consecrate to LOVE, in ages flown, —
